text.title: GEO/AEO Implementation and Agent Workflow
text.summary: GEO audit criteria, schema deployment workflow, monitoring approach, and agent-specific gotchas.

section.title: GEO Audit Criteria

rule.statement: Audit each content page on five criteria before publishing or updating. Fail on any criterion triggers a specific fix, not a general "improve content" note.
rule.rationale: Vague audit feedback ("make it better") produces no action. Specific fail criteria produce specific fixes that can be verified and retested.

list.item: 1. Does each heading have a direct answer within 2 sentences? (Pass/Fail)
2. Are there FAQ sections with question-format headings? (Pass/Fail)
3. Is entity language consistent (brand name, product names, author name)? (Pass/Fail)
4. Are there tables, bullet points, or numbered lists for key data? (Pass/Fail)
5. Is there original data, framework, or insight an AI would cite? (Pass/Fail)

section.reference: LLM prompt to run GEO/AEO audit on a content page

rule.statement: Validate all JSON-LD schema with Google Rich Results Test before deployment — never deploy agent-generated schema without validation.
rule.rationale: Agents produce syntactically plausible but semantically incorrect schema: wrong @type values, missing required fields, incorrect nesting. Invalid schema produces no rich results and may trigger Search Console errors.

list.item: Generate schema from template or agent
Test with Google Rich Results Test
Verify no conflicting schema definitions on the page
Deploy and monitor Search Console for errors
Otterly.AI or Profound: monthly AI citation check (paid)
Google Alerts for brand name: free proxy for citation monitoring
Google Search Console: AI Overviews impression data (limited)
Semrush AI Visibility Toolkit: AI share of voice tracking (paid)

antipattern.description: Agent recommends "publish more content" without specifying content gaps as article titles and target queries.
antipattern.reason: Vague content volume recommendations are not actionable. Require the agent to output specific gaps as: article title, target query, and format (FAQ, HowTo, comparison).

antipattern.description: Agent updates publication dates in metadata without updating the actual content.
antipattern.reason: AI engines and Google both detect content freshness via substantive changes, not metadata timestamps. A date update without content change provides no citation benefit.

antipattern.description: Agent suggests entity relationships (brand associated with specific concepts or people) without verifying what AI engines currently associate with the brand.
antipattern.reason: Suggested entity relationships may not match the brand's current knowledge graph representation. Incorrect associations can confuse AI engine entity resolution.
